title: Generating 3D Objects from Text
concept: 3D generation aims to create full 3D models (callback to Unit 174) directly from a text description, without ever capturing real photos.

exercises.prompt: What do you think it means to 'generate' a 3D object?

options.text: Create a brand-new 3D model that didn't exist before
Take a photo of a real object from many angles
Print a flat picture on paper

exercises.explain: Generating means creating something new — in this case, a computer builds a full 3D shape from scratch, often just from a text description!

cards.title: Type Words, Get a 3D Model!
cards.body: Imagine typing 'a cute robot dog' and watching a full 3D model of it appear on your screen — one you can spin around and view from every angle. No camera, no sculpting by hand. That's the magic of 3D generation!

cards.title: What is 3D Generation?
cards.body: 3D generation is when AI creates a complete 3D model directly from a text description, like 'a wooden chair' or 'a dragon.' Unlike 3D scanning, it never needs real photos or real objects — the AI imagines and builds the shape entirely from words.

cards.title: Like a Sculptor Who Never Saw the Object
cards.body: Picture a sculptor who has never seen a dragon in real life, but has read thousands of descriptions and seen thousands of drawings of dragons. When you ask for 'a dragon,' they carve one purely from imagination and knowledge — that's exactly what AI does with 3D generation!

cards.title: From Text to Game Character
cards.body: A game designer types 'a friendly cartoon monster with big eyes.' The AI generates a full 3D model of that monster in seconds — ready to be dropped into a video game, rotated, or animated. No sculpting tools, no cameras needed.

cards.title: Myth: It Just Copies a Photo
cards.body:
Myth: 3D generation just turns an existing photo into a 3D shape.

Truth: True text-to-3D generation never uses a real photo at all! The AI builds the entire 3D object purely from its learned knowledge of what things look like, starting only from your words.

cards.title: Scanning vs. Generating
cards.body: These two ways of getting a 3D model are very different — one starts with reality, the other starts with imagination.

left.text: Takes many real photos of a real object and rebuilds its 3D shape from them

right.text: Starts with only a text description and imagines a brand-new 3D model, no photos needed

exercises.prompt: What is the main input used to create a model in 3D generation?

options.text: A set of real photos taken from many angles

exercises.explain: 3D generation starts from words — a text prompt describing what you want — and the AI imagines and builds the 3D shape from there.

exercises.prompt: True or False: 3D generation always requires real photos of the object to create a 3D model.
exercises.explain: False! That's the whole point of 3D generation — it never needs real photos. It creates the model purely from a text description.

exercises.sentence: 3D generation creates a full 3D model directly from a ___ description, without ever using real photos.
exercises.explain: The AI uses only the text description you type — like 'a wooden chair' — to imagine and build the entire 3D object.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best shows true 3D generation in action?

options.text: Typing 'a small blue teapot' and the AI creates a brand-new 3D teapot model from scratch
Taking 50 photos of a real teapot from different angles to rebuild it in 3D
Scanning a teapot with a laser scanner to capture its exact shape

exercises.explain: True 3D generation starts only from a text prompt and imagines the object — no real teapot or photos involved, unlike scanning methods.

exercises.prompt: A student says: '3D generation and 3D scanning (from Unit 174) are basically the same thing, since both give you a 3D model.' What's the key difference they're missing?

options.text: 3D generation creates models from text descriptions with no real photos, while 3D scanning captures real photos of a real object to rebuild its shape
3D scanning is done by AI, but 3D generation is done by hand
There is no real difference — they always use the same input data
3D generation only works on objects that already exist in real life

exercises.explain: The key difference is the starting point: 3D scanning rebuilds a REAL object using real photos, while 3D generation IMAGINES a brand-new object purely from a text description — no photos or real object required!
